# sumi

> Lightweight terminal system monitor for macOS, Linux, and Raspberry Pi — single static binary, TOML config, 6 built-in color themes.

## Overview

sumi is a zero-dependency Go CLI that renders live system metrics in a bento-grid TUI. It collects CPU usage (aggregate + per-core), memory, swap, disk (with real-time I/O KB/s), network Rx/Tx (with sparkline history), top processes (with Docker/k8s container badges), thermal data, battery status, and GPU info, then renders them in colored cards using ANSI TrueColor. An NDJSON streaming mode (`--watch --renderer json`) makes it pipe-friendly for `jq`/Grafana/Prometheus. All appearance settings are configurable via CLI flags or a TOML config file.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install pre-built binary (macOS Apple Silicon example)
curl -L https://github.com/denisfl/sumi/releases/latest/download/sumi-darwin-arm64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv sumi /usr/local/bin/

# Or build from source (requires Go 1.22+)
git clone https://github.com/denisfl/sumi.git
cd sumi
go build -o sumi ./cmd/monitor

# Run single snapshot
sumi

# Watch mode, gruvbox theme, sharp borders
sumi --watch --interval 3 --theme gruvbox --border sharp

# Generate default config file
sumi --init-config

# List available themes
sumi --list-themes
```

## Key Concepts

- **Snapshot**: A point-in-time capture of all system metrics (`internal/model.Snapshot`). Collected once per render cycle.
- **Collector**: Platform-specific goroutine that fills a `Snapshot`. Darwin and Linux have separate implementations with build tags. Stateful collectors (disk I/O, battery) store previous samples between ticks for delta calculations.
- **Renderer**: Consumes a `Snapshot` and writes output. Two renderers: `tui` (default, ANSI TrueColor) and `json`/`ndjson` (compact per-line JSON for streaming).
- **Theme**: A set of 10 RGB colors (border, title, text, green, yellow, red, cyan, purple, orange, teal) loaded from embedded TOML files. User themes override built-ins.
- **BoxChars**: A set of 8 Unicode box-drawing characters for card borders. Four styles: rounded, sharp, double, bold.
- **Config**: TOML file at `~/.config/sumi/config.toml`. CLI flags always override config file values.
- **History Rings**: Fixed-capacity ring buffers (`internal/history.Ring`) updated each tick. Power sparklines for CPU, Mem, Net Rx, Net Tx (global, cap 120) and per-PID CPU/Mem (cap 60).
- **Detail Panel**: Per-process detail overlay rendered after the main view. Shows PPID, threads, FD count, cwd, start time, and CPU/Mem sparklines. Opened with the `d` key.

## CLI Flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-w`, `--watch` | false | Continuous watch mode |
| `-n`, `--interval int` | 5 | Refresh interval in seconds |
| `--renderer string` | `tui` | Output renderer: `tui` or `json` |
| `--compact` | false | Compact 4-line card layout |
| `--theme string` | `tokyo-night` | Color theme name |
| `--border string` | `rounded` | Border style: `rounded`, `sharp`, `double`, `bold` |
| `--list-themes` | — | Print available theme names and exit |
| `--init-config` | — | Write annotated default config to `~/.config/sumi/config.toml` and exit |
| `--version` | — | Print version and exit |

## Config File

Location: `~/.config/sumi/config.toml` (or `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sumi/config.toml`)

Generate with: `sumi --init-config`

```toml
interval = 5
renderer = "tui"
theme = "tokyo-night"
border_style = "rounded"
compact_mode = false
widgets = ["thermal", "cpu", "memory", "disk", "network", "processes", "system"]
```

All keys are optional — missing keys use defaults. CLI flags always override config file values.

## Built-in Themes

- `tokyo-night` — default dark theme (blue/purple palette)
- `gruvbox` — warm retro colors
- `catppuccin-mocha` — pastel dark theme
- `nord` — arctic, bluish palette
- `dracula` — dark purple/pink palette
- `one-dark` — Atom One Dark palette

## Custom Theme Format

Place at `~/.config/sumi/themes/<name>.toml`. User themes take precedence over built-ins.

```toml
name = "my-theme"

[border]
r = 86; g = 95; b = 137

[title]
r = 122; g = 162; b = 247

[text]
r = 192; g = 202; b = 245

[green]
r = 158; g = 206; b = 106

[yellow]
r = 224; g = 175; b = 104

[red]
r = 247; g = 118; b = 142

[cyan]
r = 125; g = 207; b = 255

[purple]
r = 187; g = 154; b = 247

[orange]
r = 255; g = 158; b = 100

[teal]
r = 42; g = 195; b = 222
```

## JSON / NDJSON Output

```bash
# Single snapshot JSON
sumi --renderer json

# NDJSON stream (one object per line) — pipe-friendly
sumi --watch --renderer json | jq '.CPU.Usage'
```

Useful fields in the JSON object:

```json
{
  "Timestamp": "2026-04-01T18:42:05Z",
  "Platform": "darwin",
  "Hostname": "macbook",
  "Uptime": "2d 03:14:22",
  "CPU": { "Usage": 34.2, "Cores": 10, "CoreUsages": [45.1, 22.3] },
  "Mem": { "TotalBytes": 17179869184, "UsedBytes": 9663676416, "FreeBytes": 7516192768, "SwapTotal": 2147483648, "SwapUsed": 536870912 },
  "Disks": [{ "MountPoint": "/", "TotalBytes": 494384795648, "UsedBytes": 214748364800, "FreeBytes": 279636430848, "ReadKBps": 128.4, "WriteKBps": 32.1 }],
  "Net": { "Interface": "en0", "IP": "192.168.1.10", "RxKBps": 18.4, "TxKBps": 2.1 },
  "Thermal": { "TempC": 55.2, "ArmFreqMHz": 0, "GpuFreqMHz": 0, "Throttled": "" },
  "Battery": { "ChargePct": 87.0, "Charging": false, "TimeLeft": "2h 14m" },
  "Procs": [{ "Name": "chrome", "PID": 1234, "CPUPct": 22.1, "MemPct": 3.4, "Container": "" }],
  "History": { "CPUSpark": "▁▂▃▅▇", "MemSpark": "▃▃▄▄▅", "NetRxSpark": "▁▁▂▄▇", "NetTxSpark": "▁▁▁▂▁" }
}
```

## Project Layout

```
cmd/monitor/main.go          — entry point, flag parsing, theme loading, per-PID rings, detail panel
internal/
  collector/
    darwin.go                — macOS: top, netstat, osx-cpu-temp, sysctl, iostat (disk I/O), pmset (battery)
    linux.go                 — Linux: /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, /sys/class/net, /proc/diskstats (disk I/O), /sys/class/power_supply (battery), cgroup container detection
    procdetail_darwin.go     — macOS ReadProcDetail via ps + lsof
    procdetail_linux.go      — Linux ReadProcDetail via /proc/PID/{status,fd,cwd,stat}
    rpi.go                   — Raspberry Pi extras: vcgencmd, thermal zone
    collector.go             — Collector interface
  config/
    config.go                — Config struct, Load(), Default(), DefaultConfigContent
  model/
    snapshot.go              — Snapshot, CPU, Mem, DiskInfo (w/ ReadKBps/WriteKBps), Net, Thermal, ProcEntry (w/ Container), BatteryInfo, ProcDetail structs
  renderer/
    renderer.go              — Renderer interface, New(cfg, theme, box) factory
    tui.go                   — TUI renderer: card layout, progress bars, color helpers, RenderDetailPanel()
    json.go                  — JSON renderer + ndjsonRenderer (compact per-line)
  theme/
    theme.go                 — Theme struct, Color, BoxChars, Load(), ListBuiltin(), BoxStyle()
    themes/*.toml            — Embedded built-in theme files
  history/
    ring.go                  — Ring buffer + Sparkline() method
```

## Platform Requirements

| Platform | Notes |
|---|---|
| macOS (Apple Silicon) | Temperature requires building `smctemp` from source: https://github.com/narugit/smctemp |
| macOS (Intel) | Temperature requires `brew install osx-cpu-temp` (or `smctemp`) |
| Linux x86_64 | No extra dependencies; container badge detection via `/proc/PID/cgroup` |
| Raspberry Pi arm64 / armv7 | Full thermal via `vcgencmd` (Raspberry Pi OS) |

## Interactive Keybindings (watch mode)

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| `q` / `Ctrl+C` | Quit |
| `v` | Toggle compact / full layout |
| `t` | Cycle to next theme |
| `j` / `k` | Move selection down / up in Top Processes |
| `s` | Toggle sort column: CPU% ↔ MEM% |
| `d` | Open process detail panel; press again or `Esc` to close |
| `Enter` | Prompt to SIGTERM selected process |

## Common LLM Tasks

**"Change the default theme"**: Edit `cfg.Theme` default in `internal/config/config.go` and `theme = "..."` in `DefaultConfigContent`.

**"Add a new theme"**: Create `internal/theme/themes/<name>.toml` with the 10 RGB color sections shown above. The `//go:embed themes/*.toml` directive picks it up automatically at build time.

**"Add a new card"**: Add a field to `internal/model/snapshot.go`, collect data in `darwin.go`/`linux.go`, implement `renderXxxCard()` in `tui.go`, add it to the `renderFull()` call sequence and the default `widgets` list in `config.go`.

**"Add a new flag"**: Declare with `flag.String/Bool/Int` in `main.go` after `flag.Parse()`, override the corresponding `cfg` field, pass updated values to `renderer.New(cfg, t, bc)`.

**"Understand disk I/O"**: `darwinCollector`/`linuxCollector` are stateful structs with `sync.Mutex` + previous-sample maps. `applyDiskIO()` diffs against the stored sample each tick. On darwin: `iostat -I -d -c 1`. On Linux: `/proc/diskstats` sectors * 512 bytes / elapsed time.

**"Understand battery card"**: `collectBatteryDarwin()` parses `pmset -g batt`. `linuxBattery()` reads `/sys/class/power_supply/*/type` == `"Battery"`, then `capacity`, `status`, `current_now`, `charge_now`. Returns nil on desktop/server (no battery directory).

**"Understand container badges"**: `procContainer(pid)` reads `/proc/PID/cgroup` lines. Strings `docker-` or `/docker/` → `"docker"`; `kubepods` → `"k8s"`. Empty for bare-metal.

**"Understand process detail panel"**: `collector.ReadProcDetail(pid)` is platform-specific (build-tag files). In `main.go` watch loop, `detailPID` tracks the open panel, `pidCPURings`/`pidMemRings` accumulate history. `renderer.RenderDetailPanel(det)` is a standalone exported function (not part of Renderer interface) called after `rdr.Render(snap)`.

- Repository: https://github.com/denisfl/sumi
- Issues: https://github.com/denisfl/sumi/issues
- Releases: https://github.com/denisfl/sumi/releases
- Author: https://bsky.app/profile/fedosov.me
